dimensions.label: Multi-Agent Orchestration Rigor

outcomes.description: Council session state persistence + anonymous peer review (GAP 1) + typed persona protocol (GAP 2) + checkpoint/resume (GAP 3) — 14 tests on real hardware.
Council debate engine — structured deliberation after FAIL/SPLIT verdict, builder rebuttal, judge re-evaluation with persona protocol.
council-session-state.ts is imported by the production council-parallel command path.
Multi-receipt T7: council judge fix + council debate -- multi-agent orchestration with anonymous review and persona verdicts proven.
CouncilSlot expansion: 3×4 slot grid, groupBySlot, pickJudgeSlots, cross-member file-claim rules. Enables 12 parallel worktrees.
Streaming FIFO judge queue: cross-member enforcement, drainPending, PASS/FAIL status transitions.
K-of-M weighted consensus: cross-member votes 1.0 weight, passFraction, INSUFFICIENT/SPLIT handling.
T7 multi-receipt: hierarchical council (slot expansion + streaming judge queue + K-of-M consensus) proven end-to-end.

evidence_integrity.scoreCapApplied: 6.5 cap: orchestration tests use fake/offline dispatch seams; no real parallel multi-agent execution is proven.
evidence_integrity.endToEndWorkflowVerified: No production-real multi-agent E2E; matrix-kernel status smoke is real but not orchestration.

integrityAudit.scoringScriptVerdict: Evidence input, not authority: it maps latest passed outcomes to tier scores, but does not prove realism, absence of mocks, or user-path E2E execution.
integrityAudit.rule: Scores above 8 require real end-to-end capability; scores above 7 require no material mocks/fakes in the critical path.
